name: Sourdough Pancakes-2
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the active starter, eggs, powdered milk, and brown sugar in a bowl until well combined.
2. Gradually add water or whole wheat flour to achieve the desired batter consistency. Adjust as needed for thinness or thickness.
3. Stir in baking soda and allow the mixture to rest for about one minute before cooking.
4. Heat a griddle (cast iron works best) over medium heat and lightly oil it.
5. Pour or ladle batter onto the hot griddle, using approximately 1/4 cup per pancake.
6. Cook until bubbles form on the surface of the batter and the edges start to look set (about 2-3 minutes).
7. Flip the pancakes and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until golden brown on both sides.
Variations:
1. Add a cup of blueberries or other fruit before cooking.
2. Experiment with adding buckwheat, cracked wheat, wheat germ, etc.
3. Omit brown sugar and add 1 cup granola for extra-nutty pancakes.
Waffles:
1. Combine the batter as directed above and replace the oil in the waffle iron recipe with an additional 1/4 cup vegetable oil.
